name: Basic White Sauce with Milk (Bechamel)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, combine the milk, chopped onion, grated carrot, nutmeg, and white pepper.
2. Heat over medium heat until just simmering, then remove from heat.
3. Cover and allow to steep for ten minutes.
4. Strain the warm milk through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing on the vegetables to extract juices.
5. Let the strained milk cool to room temperature.
6. In a separate bowl, mix together the instant nonfat dry milk, cornstarch or arrowroot, and flour.
7. Gradually add small amounts of the cooled milk to the dry mixture, stirring continuously until smooth.
8. Return the smooth milk mixture to the saucepan.
9. Stir over medium heat until the sauce boils and thickens slightly.
10. If not using immediately, cover and refrigerate. The sauce will thicken further in cold storage; thin with a little flavored cooking juice, wine, or stock if needed.
11. Makes about 1 cup; recipe can be easily doubled.
Vegetables steeped in hot milk add flavor to this versatile sauce, which can replace butter-flour-milk white sauce in various recipes. It also serves as the base for a salt-free cheese sauce that can enhance vegetables, open-faced sandwiches, macaroni dishes, or baked potatoes.
